items.headline: Auto Sales Dealership Proposed for El Camino Real
items.summary: Planning Commission to consider a special development permit allowing auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The project involves lot improvements like repaving and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: City launches financial assistance pilot for water customers
items.summary: San José is implementing a pilot program to provide financial assistance to water customers, likely aimed at helping residents manage utility costs during the ongoing drought and affordability challenges in the region.

items.headline: City in litigation over Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a closed session on May 12 to discuss potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The city is also negotiating real property matters related to properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission to review 27-unit townhome project with affordable housing
items.summary: The Commission is scheduled to consider a residential project comprising 27 three-story townhomes, with 5 units designated as affordable. This mixed-income development represents a significant residential addition to Cupertino's housing stock.

items.headline: Bloom Energy Tower 3 data center permitted at Great Oaks
items.summary: A $13 million permit was issued to complete Bloom Energy's Tower 3 data center facility at 5 Great Oaks Boulevard. The project represents a significant commercial investment in San José's tech infrastructure.

items.headline: City Council to hear 72-unit affordable housing project at Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The Council is holding a public hearing on a proposal to build a six-story, 100% affordable housing development with 72 units on a 0.43-acre city-owned parking lot (Lot T) at 450 Lytton Avenue. The project requests density bonus concessions under state law to make the housing financially feasible on the small parcel.

items.headline: New electrical infrastructure permitted for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: Palo Alto has issued a permit for significant electrical upgrades at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development tenant improvements, including new transformers and extended conduit along Porter Drive. This signals growing demand for power-intensive R&D operations in the area.

items.headline: Council adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: Campbell City Council passed a resolution establishing a policy that prohibits city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement activities. This limits federal agencies' ability to conduct certain operations on municipal property.
